questions.question: What is the primary role of a 'Pillar Page' in a content architecture?

options.text: To be a short, daily update on news
To act as a comprehensive, 'hub' guide that covers a broad topic in depth
To show your product's pricing
To link to your competitors' websites

questions.explanation: Pillar pages are high-level hubs (2,000-5,000 words) that establish your definitive authority on a broad topic.

questions.question: Which three factors should intersect when selecting your content pillars?

options.text: Low cost, high speed, and ease of writing
Your expertise, an urgent ICP problem, and search volume/intent
Visual design, video quality, and audio clarity
LinkedIn trends, Twitter hashtags, and Reddit memes

questions.explanation: A strong pillar must be something you can teach well (Expertise), something your customers care about (Pain), and something people are searching for (Intent).

questions.question: What is the benefit of a 'Long-Tail' keyword strategy for a solo founder?

options.text: It targets more people at once
It allows you to compete with multi-billion dollar companies by winning on specificity
Long-tail keywords are cheaper to buy as ads
It requires less research than head terms

questions.explanation: Large companies own generic terms. Solo founders win by answering specific, niche questions that AI systems and motivated buyers actually search for.

questions.question: Roughly how many cluster articles focused on a single pillar are needed to start building 'Topical Authority'?

options.text: 1-2 articles
5 articles
15-20 articles
500 articles

questions.explanation: Consistency and depth matter. Reaching the '20-article threshold' signals to engines and audiences that you are a serious authority in that niche.

questions.question: What is 'Cluster Content' in the hub-and-spoke model?

options.text: Ads that you run on Facebook
Specific subtopic articles that link back to the main Pillar Page
A group of customers who buy at the same time
Content that you delete because it's irrelevant

questions.explanation: Clusters are the 'spokes' that provide detailed answers to specific questions while reinforcing the authority of the central hub.

questions.question: List your three proposed Content Pillars. For your primary (Pillar #1), brainstorm 3 specific Long-Tail cluster ideas. Why did you choose these specific subtopics? How do they connect to an urgent pain your ICP is feeling?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate if the pillars are too broad (e.g., 'Marketing') vs. specific (e.g., 'Lead Gen for Agencies'). Ensure the cluster ideas feel like 'Long-Tail' answers to real problems.
